Age: 25

Invalid age! Must be 0-150.
phase1.explanation:
Encapsulation is about controlling access to data:

Access Modifiers:
• public — accessible from anywhere
• private — accessible only within the class (most secure)
• protected — accessible in package + subclasses
• (default) — accessible within the package

Getters/Setters (accessors/mutators):
• Getters: return private field values
• Setters: update with validation

Why encapsulate?
1. Control — validate data before setting
2. Security — prevent invalid states
3. Maintainability — change implementation without breaking code

Key rule: Always make fields private! Use public getters/setters.

Benefits: Hide implementation details, protect data integrity.

phase2.errorMessage:
EncapsulationExample.java:11: error: name has private access in Person
        System.out.println(person.name);
                                 ^
EncapsulationExample.java:12: error: age has private access in Person
        person.age = 30;
               ^
2 errors

phase3.prompt:
Create a BankAccount class with:
• Private fields: owner (String), balance (double)
• Constructor
• Getters for both
• deposit(double amount) — adds to balance (must be positive)
• withdraw(double amount) — subtracts from balance (can't go negative)
• displayInfo() method

Test by creating an account and doing deposits/withdrawals.
dialogueHints: Fields should be private, getters public
withdraw should check if enough balance
phase3.openingDialogue: Time to lock down your data! Encapsulation is your shield~ 🛡️
